article_title: Astronomers Catch Two Planets Colliding 11,000 Light-Years Away in a Replay of the Impact That Formed Earth's Moon

content_preview.title: Astronomers Catch Two Planets Colliding 11,000 Light-Years Away in a Replay of the Impact That Formed Earth's Moon
content_preview.summary: A University of Washington team identifies signs of a catastrophic planetary collision around star Gaia20ehk, offering the first real-time analog of the giant impact that created Earth's Moon 4.5 billion years ago.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Astronomers have identified what appears to be the aftermath of a violent collision between two planets orbiting the Sun-like star Gaia20ehk, located roughly 11,000 light-years from Earth near the constellation Puppis. The findings, [published on March 11 in The Astrophysical Journal Letters](https://www.washington.edu/news/2026/03/11/uw-astronomers-spot-planet-collision-evidence/), represent one of only a handful of planetary collisions ever observed and bear a striking resemblance...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-written science news at 476 words with clear Overview/What We Know/What We Don't Know structure. Accessible to general audience while preserving scientific precision.
editor_notes.source_verification: Space.com — ✅ Confirmed: Gaia20ehk, 11,000 light-years, Puppis, debris at ~1 AU, exomoon formation possibility, Theia parallel, UW team, ApJ Letters March 11. UW News — ✅ Confirmed: Tzanidakis (doctoral candidate), Davenport (assistant research professor), 2016 dips, 2021 erratic behavior, infrared spike, 'caught this impact in real time' quote. ScienceDaily — ✅ Confirmed: Tzanidakis 'stars like our sun don't do that' quote (source says 'I can't emphasize enough that stars like our sun don't do that' — article paraphrases slightly but meaning preserved), Davenport 'If we catch more of these collisions' quote, Vera Rubin ~100 collisions estimate.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified across all three sources. The 'sensationalist language' automated finding is a false positive — the word 'incredible' appears only in a direct researcher quote ('It's incredible that various telescopes caught this impact in real time'), which is properly attributed to Tzanidakis and confirmed in ScienceDaily. Minor note: Tzanidakis quote in article is slightly condensed vs ScienceDaily's full version, but the meaning is preserved accurately.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. The article's own language avoids sensationalism; the flagged word is in a sourced quote. Moon formation parallel is presented factually without hyperbole.
editor_notes.originality: No existing planetary collision or Gaia20ehk articles in the archive. Topic is entirely new.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid science news article with all claims verified against three independent sources. The automated sensationalism flag is a false positive (word appears in researcher quote). Approved for publication.
